title: A new chart type is born - Stacked Bar!

posts.author: itaycohen-monday.com (Itay Cohen)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have just released this new chart type to the board views and dashboards!

https://global.discourse-cdn.com/monday/original/2X/8/8d45c677baa2d200972f4f3a6811d75d6b19e31b.mov

What is a stacked bar?
It’s a chart that uses bars to show comparisons between categories of data with the ability to break down and compare parts of a whole.

What can I do with it?
good question!

  1. Do you want to track your team iteration progress? to view it by the task estimations with a status breakdown?

    g11570×707 39.7 KB

  2. Do you want to analyze your sales team results? See the sales results by month and breakdown by sales rep?

    g2a1576×707 31.6 KB

  3. Do you want to create a breakdown summary of your product users by month and by status?

    g31570×702 29.1 KB

Ok, how can I do it?
Just choose “stacked bar” in the chart type dropdown, and the column you want to stack by in the new setting dropdown (as in the video).

More information:

  • It’s open to everyone
  • Currently, it’s supported for single board selection in the dashboard.

We would be happy to hear your feedback and suggestions!
Enjoy!

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: This new chart is awesome, just saying thanks

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: A post was split to a new topic: Grouping of charts by days

posts.author: KyleFox (Kyle Fox)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is exactly what I was looking for but I have a question related to it.

I was trying to create a stacked bar with the item names as the X-axis, status as the stacking, and days spent in the status as the Y-axis. At this point, count is the only Y axis option. Is this a possibility here or do people have alternate solutions for displaying the data.
